description: Null-model test for the productive-paradigm claim (Finding 1.8). Tests whether the log-frequency vs edit-1 variant count correlation exceeds a character-trigram null that lacks morphological structure.

methodology.voynich_real: Top 50 members of each family by frequency; Pearson r of log-freq vs edit-1 neighbor count within that family's top-50.
methodology.trigram_null: 10 replicates: fit character trigram model on real Voynich; generate synthetic corpus of same size; classify with same family rules; compute same correlation.
methodology.chaucer_natural: All tokens from Chaucer's Canterbury Tales; top 50 by frequency; same correlation definition. Uses ALL tokens, not family-filtered, since the EVA family definitions don't apply.

interpretation_rules.supports_productive_morphology: Voynich r >> trigram null r AND Voynich r ≈ Chaucer r
interpretation_rules.artifact_only: Voynich r ≈ trigram null r (within factor 1.3)
interpretation_rules.partial_support: Voynich r above null but below NL

QOK.verdict: artifact: correlation explained by Zipfian combinatorics (trigram null)

CHEDY.verdict: artifact: correlation explained by Zipfian combinatorics (trigram null)

AIIN.verdict: artifact: correlation explained by Zipfian combinatorics (trigram null)
